title: trinia seselioides
intro: Trinia seselioides, also known as the Lesser Mead-knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Trinia seselioides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
